response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, infrastructure experience, and teaching ability, they fall significantly short on two critical requirements: only ~6 months of C++ experience versus the required 8 years, and minimal evidence of formal technical leadership or mentoring in cloud/DevOps contexts. The diverse technology background and proven infrastructure work do not offset these fundamental gaps for a mid-senior technical lead role.
strengths: Extensive infrastructure and distributed systems experience (Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second with low-latency optimization, Altom CI/CD and deployment infrastructure)
Strong software engineering culture and teaching ability (TDD practices at RIDE, organized refactoring dojos, technical workshops, community engagement)
Proven ability to learn and apply diverse technologies across full stack (web, mobile, desktop, embedded with multiple languages and platforms)
concerns: Critical C++ experience gap: only ~6 months documented (National Instruments 2014-2015) versus required minimum of 8 years is a disqualifying miss
Minimal evidence of formal technical leadership: held Staff/Senior titles but no clear record of mentoring teams or driving technical strategy at scale
Narrow cloud platform specialization: AWS CDK and infrastructure work present but lacks deep production-scale cloud compute architecture experience
